title: Ambiguous requires-python specifier `~=3.10` (no patch version) in coordinator and worker pyproject.toml
summary: Both charm packages declare `requires-python = "~=3.10"` (coordinator/pyproject.toml:5 and worker/pyproject.toml:6). PEP 440's tilde operator without a patch version is interpreted as `>=3.10,<4`, NOT the apparently-intended `>=3.10,<3.11`. uv emits a warning about exactly this on every tox run. Either the intent is `>=3.10` (use that explicit form) or `~=3.10.0` (to pin to 3.10.x). The ambiguous form should be replaced with an explicit, unambiguous specifier in both files.
why_good_first_issue: Two-line mechanical edit in two sibling files, no behaviour change, and the fix is self-evidently correct. The build tool (uv) already surfaces the exact warning, so a newcomer can verify the before/after by running `tox -e lint` and seeing the warning disappear. No charm/cluster knowledge required; great first contribution to learn the repo's tox/uv flow.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/loki-operators && grep -n "requires-python" coordinator/pyproject.toml worker/pyproject.toml' ; multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/loki-operators/coordinator && export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e lint 2>&1 | head'
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection + tooling. grep shows `coordinator/pyproject.toml:5:requires-python = "~=3.10"` and `worker/pyproject.toml:6:requires-python = "~=3.10"`. Running `tox -e lint` in coordinator/ prints: warning: The `requires-python` specifier (`~=3.10`) in `loki-coordinator-k8s` uses the tilde specifier (`~=`) without a patch version. This will be interpreted as `>=3.10, <4`. Did you mean `~=3.10.0` ... We recommend only using the tilde specifier with a patch version to avoid ambiguity. So the tool confirms the specifier is ambiguous. (lint otherwise passes: 'All checks passed!').
fix_hint: Decide intent: if any Python >=3.10 is supported, change both lines to `requires-python = ">=3.10"`; if only 3.10.x, use `requires-python = "~=3.10.0"`. Given the charms run on modern bases, `>=3.10` is the most likely correct intent. Apply identically to coordinator/pyproject.toml and worker/pyproject.toml. Re-run `tox -e lint` in each to confirm the uv warning is gone.
notes: Not an existing tracker issue; self-identified during repro. Both charms in this monorepo share the same defect. Verified at repo HEAD (depth-80 clone). Trivially confirmable without a live deployment.

title: Loki saves invalid LogQL rule to disk, `lokitool` fails to sync ALL rules because of one invalid rule
suggested_labels: Type: Bug; Status: Triage; good first issue
summary: The coordinator's `_set_alerts` (coordinator/src/charm.py:272) writes EVERY alert rule file received from consumers to disk via `_push_alert_rules` (charm.py:242), then runs a single `lokitool rules sync <all files> --address=... --id=fake` (charm.py:~295-309). lokitool sync is atomic: if any one rule file is invalid, the whole sync fails and NO rules get loaded. The coordinator does no per-rule validation before writing/syncing; it only logs lokitool's stderr and returns (charm.py:~315). Upstream, the loki_push_api provider is supposed to validate rules with cos-tool and only persist valid ones, but invalid rules still reach disk, so one bad rule from one consumer takes down rule-loading for all consumers.
why_good_first_issue: Tightly-scoped, single method (`_set_alerts`) with a clear, well-documented failure mode in the issue. A beginner can add resilience without deep distributed-systems knowledge: e.g. validate each rules file (or sync them individually / skip-and-warn on the offending file) so one bad rule no longer blocks the rest. Good intro to the charm's alert-handling flow and to ops Pebble exec. The exact code path is small and localized.
affected_files: coordinator/src/charm.py (_set_alerts at :272, _push_alert_rules at :242, lokitool sync exec ~:295-309)
repro_steps: Full repro needs a live deploy (issue provides a bundle): deploy loki-coordinator-k8s + loki-worker-k8s + cos-configuration-k8s pointed at a repo containing one invalid LogQL rule (github.com/sinapah/cos-config), relate cos-config:loki-config -> loki:logging, then observe `juju debug-log` shows lokitool sync failing for ALL rules. Inspection of the code path (no deploy): multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/loki-operators && sed -n "242,320p" coordinator/src/charm.py'.
repro_result: LIVE-REPRODUCED 2026-06-16 (gfi-repro VM; full loki coordinator+worker+minio+s3 stack): ran the coordinator's exact `lokitool rules sync valid.yaml invalid.yaml --id=fake` -> exit 1, 'sync operation unsuccessful, unable to parse rules files' (one invalid LogQL rule aborts the WHOLE batch, valid rule dropped); valid-only sync proceeds ('creating group valid_group'). Confirms one bad rule blocks all. || needs-live-deploy (flagged for orchestrator). Code-path confirmed by inspection at HEAD: `_push_alert_rules` (charm.py:242) loops over all alert dicts and `self._push(path, rules)` writes each to RULES_DIR with NO validation; `_set_alerts` then calls `lokitool rules sync *rules_file_paths --address=... --id=fake` in a single pebble exec (charm.py ~:296-309). On nonzero/stderr output it just `logger.error(...)` and `return`s without persisting the hash (charm.py ~:313-315) -- i.e. all-or-nothing, exactly matching the reported atomic-failure behaviour. The validation gap the issue asks about lives in the consumer-side loki_push_api lib (hosted in loki-k8s-operator), but the atomic-sync amplification and the practical mitigation point are here in the coordinator.
fix_hint: In `_set_alerts`/`_push_alert_rules` add resilience to a single bad rule: either (a) validate each rules file before writing (e.g. via cos-tool / lokitool check) and skip+warn on invalid ones, or (b) run `lokitool rules sync` per-file so one failure doesn't block the others, or (c) at minimum surface WHICH file failed in the BlockedStatus/log. Note the true root cause (invalid rules reaching disk) is in charms.loki_k8s.v1.loki_push_api in loki-k8s-operator; consider a cross-repo note. Keep the hash-persist-only-on-success behaviour.
notes: Open, labelled Type: Bug + Status: Triage. Reproduction bundle is in the issue. Distinct from #66 (S3 ca_file), which I verified is ALREADY FIXED at HEAD by PR #65 / commit 19b71e1 (2026-05-15): _s3_storage_config (loki_config.py:289-290) now renders ca_file and is shared by BOTH common.storage.s3 (loki_config.py:128) and storage_config.aws (loki_config.py:307) -- so #66 is obsolete and was excluded. #100 (S3 XAmzContentSHA256Mismatch) is an S3-backend interaction, not a beginner code fix.
